title: 10 best herbal decoctions for psoriasis
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for psoriasis are a traditional remedy that has been used to alleviate symptoms of the chronic skin condition. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs in hot water, releasing their active compounds and anti-inflammatory properties. Turmeric, chamomile, and oatmeal decoctions have shown promise in reducing redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis. By using these herbal decoctions, individuals with psoriasis can experience relief from discomfort and embarrassment, allowing them to live more confidently and comfortably.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with psoriasis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and redness associated with the condition. The decoctions also have antioxidant properties, which help to repair damaged skin cells and promote collagen production, leading to improved skin elasticity and flexibility. Additionally, aloe vera has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of psoriasis. By using herbal aloe decoctions topically, individuals with psoriasis may experience relief from itching, scaling, and redness, promoting healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal aloe decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the skin, which is a hallmark symptom of psoriasis. This reduces redness, swelling, and pain associated with the condition.

Antioxidant property: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms and promote chronic inflammation.

Immunomodulatory property: Herbal aloe decoctions contain immunomodulatory compounds that help regulate the immune system's response to psoriasis, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting a balanced immune response.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound has anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative properties, which help reduce inflammation and slow down the growth of skin cells that contribute to psoriasis lesions.

Chrysophanic acid: This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects, which help reduce inflammation, prevent the activation of immune cells, and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that contribute to psoriasis.

Acemannan: This polysaccharide has immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which help regulate the immune response, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing of skin lesions in psoriasis patients.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high gel content, which helps soothe and calm skin irritations associated with psoriasis.
Stems: Used as they contain a high concentration of aloe-emodin, a compound that may help reduce inflammation and prevent cell proliferation.
Roots: Used as they contain a compound called aloin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and anti-itch properties, helping to alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a mature aloe vera leaf and carefully extract 30ml of fresh gel from it.
Combine the extracted gel with 200ml of boiling water in a saucepan to prepare the decoction.
Boil the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes over low heat to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine mesh sieve into a clean glass container to remove the solids.
Store the herbal aloe vera decoction in the refrigerator for up to 2 days before consumption.

Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.
Store the decoction in a refrigerator at 4-8 degrees celsius for up to 3 days or freeze for longer storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids work together to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, which is a common symptom of psoriasis. The decoction also contains silicic acid, which has been shown to improve skin elasticity and reduce scaling. As a result, Equisetum arvense decoctions may help to alleviate the symptoms of psoriasis, promoting healthier and more balanced skin. Regular use may lead to improved skin condition.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and improve skin conditions by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which are involved in the development of psoriasis symptoms.

Saponins: Saponins in Equisetum arvense decoctions may help in reducing inflammation and improving skin conditions by modulating the immune system and reducing oxidative stress, thereby alleviating psoriasis symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, may exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing the severity of psoriasis symptoms by suppressing inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of equisetic acid and other compounds believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Stems are used for their equisetic acid content, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with psoriasis.
Leaves: Leaves are used in decoctions for their supposed anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in soothing and calming skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 grams of equisetum arvense rhizomes and roots with a sharp knife or spade in early spring.
Wash the harvested equisetum arvense rhizomes and roots thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned equisetum arvense rhizomes and roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or cleaver.
Combine the chopped equisetum arvense rhizomes and roots with 1 liter of boiling water in a saucepan to create decoction.
Simmer the equisetum arvense decoction over low heat for 5-10 minutes to release its medicinal properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa decoctions helps with psoriasis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The decoctions have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing, which is beneficial for psoriasis sufferers. The antioxidants present in Avena sativa also help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the calming effects of the decoctions can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for psoriasis flare-ups, thereby contributing to overall skin health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and redness associated with psoriasis.
Gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA): As a neurotransmitter, GABA helps regulate nerve activity and has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often linked to psoriasis flare-ups.

Ferulic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote wound healing, making it beneficial for psoriasis treatment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Avena sativa roots are commonly used to make decoctions for psoriasis due to their high concentration of saponins and flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Avena sativa leaves are utilized to make decoctions for psoriasis because they are rich in avenanthramides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve skin symptoms.
Seeds: Avena sativa seeds are often used in decoctions for psoriasis due to their high content of fatty acids, particularly oleic acid, which may help soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Collect 1-2 teaspoons of dried avena sativa roots and stems from a trusted source or grow your own.
Measure out 1 quart of boiling water and pour it over the avena sativa in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the avena sativa for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to remove solids.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature or refrigerate for later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The gel contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that reduce inflammation and promote healing. Aloe also has antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, its moisturizing properties help to hydrate and soften the skin, reducing dryness and scaling associated with psoriasis, promoting a healthier and more balanced skin environment. This leads to improved skin texture and reduced symptoms of psoriasis.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: Help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing by stimulating the production of growth factors.
Anthraquinones (Chrysophanic acid and Aloe-emodin): Exert anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative effects, reducing the severity of psoriatic lesions.
Aliphatic glycosides (Aloin and Aloe-borixin): Inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which contribute to the development of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel extracted from Aloe barbadensis leaves is used to treat psoriasis due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Stems: The inner pulp of Aloe barbadensis stems is used to make decoctions, which help in reducing inflammation and promoting skin health in psoriasis patients.
Roots: Aloe barbadensis roots are used to make decoctions that help in reducing itching and inflammation associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh aloe barbadensis leaves from a mature plant in the early morning.
Wash the harvested leaves with cool water and then gently scrub them with a soft brush to remove dirt.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and soak them in a bowl of cool water for 30 minutes to rehydrate the plant material.
Boil 2-3 cups of water in a pot and then reduce heat to a simmer for 20-30 minutes to create a decoction base.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera decoctions help with psoriasis because they possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm irritated skin. By promoting skin regeneration and reducing inflammation, Aloe vera decoctions can provide relief and improve the overall appearance of psoriasis-affected skin. remedy_constituents: Apolipids: They help reduce inflammation and promote skin repair, which is beneficial for soothing psoriasis symptoms.
Glucomannans: These polysaccharides help modulate the immune response, reducing the severity of psoriasis symptoms.
Anthraquinones: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate psoriasis symptoms and promote skin health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside the leaves is widely used due to its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which help soothe psoriasis symptoms.
Stems: The sap or latex from the stems is used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce redness and irritation associated with psoriasis.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing in psoriasis patients.
remedy_recipe: Choose fresh aloe vera leaves with vibrant green color and firm texture for maximum potency.
Cut off 1-2 inches from the tip of aloe leaves to expose the gel inside.
Scoop out 2-4 tablespoons of the clear aloe vera gel from the leaf into a bowl.
Combine aloe vera gel with 2 cups of water and bring to a boil in a saucepan.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 15-20 minutes before straining.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum decoctions helps with psoriasis because they contain a high concentration of hyperforin and hypericin, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. By promoting skin health and reducing inflammation, herbal hypericum perforatum decoctions offer a natural and effective treatment option for managing psoriasis symptoms. Regular use may lead to improved skin clarity and reduced flare-ups.
remedy_constituents: Hypericin: This naphthodianthrone constituent exhibits potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with psoriasis.
Hyperforin: As a phloroglucinol derivative, hyperforin has demonstrated anti-oxidative and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to the alleviation of psoriasis symptoms.

Quercetin: This flavonoid compound has shown anti-inflammatory and anti-proliferative effects, which can help reduce the severity of psoriasis by inhibiting the growth of skin cells and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers are also frequently used in decoctions due to their high flavonoid content, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
Roots: The roots are another commonly used part for their ability to provide sustained relief from psoriasis symptoms, attributed to their content of glycosides and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers in a clean container for herbal decoction.
Combine the dried flowers with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container to remove the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before storing it in the refrigerator for later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The licorice root extracts in these decoctions have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, which are key factors in the development and progression of psoriasis. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing, Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions can help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis such as redness, itching, and scaling. This makes it a promising natural remedy for managing psoriasis and improving skin health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.

Flavonoids (specifically, licoisoflavones and licoflavones): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms and prevent tissue damage.

Triterpene saponins (specifically, glycyrrhizic acid and its derivatives): They exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects, which can help to reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms and prevent the progression of the disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which is known to have anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties.
Leaves: They are used as an alternative to roots, as they also contain glycyrrhizin, which helps in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of psoriasis.
Barks: They are sometimes used as a substitute for roots, but with less potency and efficacy in treating psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a trusted supplier or garden.
Wash the dried roots thoroughly with filtered water to remove any debris or impurities.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder.
Combine the powdered roots with 1 liter of boiling water in a saucepan to create the decoction.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes before straining and cooling the liquid.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1/4 cup of dried calendula officinalis flowers and sift them to remove any debris.
Combine the sifted calendula flowers with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes allowing flavors to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
